task_completion.description: Log in to the OpenSIS application using the provided admin credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the student search or list and look for Kenny McCormick.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS 'Find a Student' screen or student list showing search criteria for 'Kenny' or 'McCormick', or displaying him in the search results.

task_completion.description: Open the student record for Kenny McCormick.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The detailed student profile, demographics, or custom fields page specifically for 'Kenny McCormick' (Grade 10).

task_completion.description: Enter the locker number '555' into the appropriate field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form field (likely a custom field for locker number) within Kenny McCormick's profile containing the inputted text '555'.

task_completion.description: Save the updated student record with the new locker number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success/confirmation message indicating the student record was saved, or the profile page reloading to display the saved locker number '555' in a read-only or updated state.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenSIS GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenSIS interface, rather than terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database commands to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible executing SQL commands (e.g., mysql, sqlite3) to update the 'students' table directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenSIS application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The locker number is entered into the genuine OpenSIS web form, not typed into a text editor, notes app, or a manipulated local HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target student: Kenny McCormick (Grade 10). Target locker number: 555. Login credentials: username 'admin', password 'Admin@123'. The locker number is a custom field in the OpenSIS student record.
